title: Question about the book Die Sterne
display_title: Question about the book Die Sterne
wikitext:
[]: Question about the book Die Sterne             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Feb 04, 2010 7:06 pm Who actually has the Evangelion artbook "Die Sterne", and, if so, is it worth getting? I always wanted that book.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Feb 04, 2010 9:17 pm It's a lovely book with metric tons of official art from every corner of promotional and merchandise lands. Nice and HUEG, has an index at the back listing when and where each pic was first used as well as the artist. If you're grabbing it grab the updated Ver 2 or whatever they call it. I own the original release and am too poor to upgrade right now :( Damn Dengekis eat up all my money

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Feb 04, 2010 9:24 pm Der Mond is the only Evangelion art book that I know of that is in English. I own it, and I love it's full colored art works and glimpses into early character designs. It even has stuff from Sadamoto's other projects. I'm not aware that Die Sterne is sold in the States. You can always check Amazon.com for reviews from others who own the book. You may need to go to the Japanese site to order a new copy.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Thu Feb 04, 2010 9:27 pm I have both Der Mond and Die Sterne. They're both awesome artbooks, and they're chock full of awesome pics. Der Mond has a lot of Sadamoto's other work, too.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Feb 04, 2010 9:36 pm Actually, I have "Der Mond" and that is, like everyone else says, an excellent book. It's too bad that "Die Sterne" isn't in the United States yet. The book would be more popular if Japan released it worldwide; AND, on top of that, it's out of stock everywhere. (Sorry, not doing the eBay thing) Also, another question: I have also believed that there are 2 versions of the book; how big of the difference is each version, and which version should I rather get (if I get a chance to buy it)?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 04, 2010 10:12 pm Die Sterne is an awesome book. As far as Eva art books go, it's one I would highly recommend. I touch on it in a series of videos I made going into the depths of my Eva shelf: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aEB_7muXF6I#t=04m12 I would probably recommend the newer version, it's supposedly the same but with more stuff added.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Fri Feb 05, 2010 12:04 am I downloaded my version, together with "der Mond". Then I realized that I had already collected most of artwork from some huge 500-MB .zip archive that I got from this site.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Feb 28, 2010 1:53 am LeoXiao wrote: I downloaded my version, together with "der Mond". Then I realized that I had already collected most of artwork from some huge 500-MB .zip archive that I got from this site.
